articles.title: Japanese Yen loses ground as Trump rejects Iran proposal ahead of US CPI
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair elevates near the 157.10 region on Monday, with the US Dollar (USD) strenghtening after United States (US) President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s latest peace proposal, calling it “totally unacceptable.”

articles.title: Japan: Wage gains, confidence risks and BoJ – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes Japanese wage data have improved, with unions securing solid ‘shunto’ wage hikes and real wages rising again, supporting the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) desired virtuous cycle.

articles.expand: Iran is ready to downblend highly enriched uranium to levels of 3.7% and 20%, according to Al Jazeera citing a source. Washington refused to transfer the highly enriched uranium to Russia and instead suggested a third country receive it. Iran rejected the transfer of Uranium stockpiles outside of Iran. articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.title: Gold steadies as markets await US CPI amid rising Middle East tensions
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) holds firm after filling the week’s bearish opening gap, supported by a softer US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD steadies as Starmer ouster fears rock UK Gilts
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) holds modestly firm against the US Dollar (USD) during the North American session on Monday, down a minimal 0.06% as pressure mounts on the UK’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer as his party sustained losses in local elections.

articles.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average steady as Iran rebuff lifts Oil, CPI in focus
articles.expand: US equity index futures opened the week little changed as Wall Street weighed a renewed flare-up in the US-Iran standoff against this week's marquee inflation print.

articles.title: Australian Dollar surges as traders await US CPI, Trump rejects Iran deal
articles.expand: AUD/USD surges toward the 0.7260 region on Monday, supported by improving demand for commodity-linked currencies despite renewed geopolitical tensions after United States (US) President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s latest peace proposal, calling it “totally unacceptable.”

articles.title: US Dollar: Gradual depreciation path outlined – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas economists expect the US economy to grow above potential in 2026, with Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at 2.4% and inflation overshooting at 3.5%. They see the Fed Funds target range steady at 3.5%-3.75% as the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) adopts a two-sided outlook.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Choppy trade versus US Dollar around intervention line – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC’s FX Christopher Wong describes USD/JPY as a two-way trade after suspected Ministry of Finance (MoF) intervention capped gains near 160. He notes bearish daily momentum but says fundamentals are not decisively Japanese Yen (JPY)-positive, with higher Oil prices still a drag.

articles.title: BoC: Patience on hikes despite oil shock – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists expect the Bank of Canada (BoC) to hold its policy rate at 2.25% through 2026 before returning to a 2.75% neutral level in 2027 via 25 bp hikes in January and March.

articles.title: US Dollar Index: DXY trading in range as inflation eyed – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chris Turner notes the Dollar has started the week slightly stronger as hopes for a Middle East ceasefire fade and Oil prices jump. He highlights US April Consumer Price Index (CPI) as the key release, with expectations for higher headline and core inflation.

articles.title: Australian Dollar: Carry appeal boosted by RBA stance – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG analysts highlight that the Australian Dollar has outperformed since the Middle East conflict, supported by higher energy prices and a hawkish Reserve Bank of Australia.

articles.title: British Pound: Growth slowdown and BoE tightening – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas economists expect United Kingdom (UK) Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth to slow to 0.7% in 2026 from 1.4% in 2025, with quarterly expansion dropping to about 0.1%. Inflation is projected to re-accelerate to 3.6% before easing only slightly.
